title: First Day Onboarding at a New Company

all.en: I woke up at six o'clock, even though my orientation did not start until nine.
all.zh: 雖然新人訓練九點才開始，我六點就醒了。

all.en: I picked out my outfit carefully, aiming for professional but not too stiff.
all.zh: 我精心挑選了服裝，希望看起來專業但又不會太拘謹。

all.en: The HR officer greeted me at the lobby and handed me a thick welcome packet.
all.zh: 人資主任在大廳迎接我，並遞給我一份厚厚的歡迎資料袋。

all.en: My new badge had my photo printed slightly crooked, but I still felt official.
all.zh: 我的新識別證上照片印得有點歪，但我仍覺得自己正式入職了。

all.en: I was introduced to so many people that their names blurred together in my mind.
all.zh: 我被介紹給太多人，他們的名字在我腦海中混成一團。

all.en: My desk was tidy, with a brand-new laptop and a small succulent plant.
all.zh: 我的辦公桌很整潔，擺著一台全新的筆電和一盆小多肉植物。

all.en: The IT technician spent an hour setting up my accounts and email.
all.zh: 資訊技術人員花了一個小時幫我設定帳號和電子郵件。

all.en: At lunch, my team took me to a noodle shop around the corner.
all.zh: 午餐時，我的團隊帶我去附近的一家麵店。

all.en: They explained the unspoken office rules, like never touching the boss's coffee mug.
all.zh: 他們向我說明一些不成文的辦公室規矩，例如絕對別碰老闆的咖啡杯。

all.en: By the afternoon, I was attending my first team meeting and taking notes furiously.
all.zh: 到了下午，我已經參加了第一場團隊會議，並拼命做筆記。

all.en: When I got home, I collapsed on the sofa, exhausted but excited for tomorrow.
all.zh: 回到家後，我癱倒在沙發上，雖然筋疲力盡，卻對明天充滿期待。

all.en: On the second morning, I arrived earlier and even managed to greet the security guard.
all.zh: 第二天早上，我到得更早，甚至還向警衛打了招呼。

all.en: My team leader handed me a list of training videos to finish by the end of the week.
all.zh: 我的組長交給我一份訓練影片清單，要求在週末前看完。

all.en: I struggled to remember the WiFi password and finally wrote it on a sticky note.
all.zh: 我一直記不住 WiFi 密碼，最後乾脆寫在便利貼上。

all.en: At eleven, my mentor invited me for a long chat about workplace expectations.
all.zh: 十一點時，我的導師邀請我長談一次關於職場期待的話題。

all.en: She told me it was fine to ask questions, since pretending to know was much riskier.
all.zh: 她告訴我發問完全沒問題，因為假裝懂反而是更大的風險。

all.en: In the afternoon, I shadowed a senior colleague on a real customer phone call.
all.zh: 下午，我跟著一位資深同事旁聽了一通真實的客戶電話。

all.en: I scribbled down phrases she used and promised to practice them before tomorrow.
all.zh: 我抄下她使用的句子，並承諾明天前一定要練習一遍。

all.en: By Friday, I could already name half of the people on my floor without panicking.
all.zh: 到了星期五，我已經能毫不慌張地叫出樓層上一半同事的名字。

all.en: My little succulent on the desk had a new friend, a tiny photo of my family.
all.zh: 我桌上的小多肉植物多了一位新朋友，那是一張小小的家人照片。

all.en: On the weekend, I told my parents that the new job felt right after all.
all.zh: 週末時，我告訴父母，這份新工作果然還是適合我。

all.en: The third week, my mentor handed me a small project to lead on my own.
all.zh: 第三週，我的導師把一個小專案交給我獨立負責。

all.en: I was both excited and slightly terrified to make my very first solo decisions.
all.zh: 我既興奮又有點害怕，要做出我第一個獨立的決定。

all.en: I scheduled meetings with three departments to gather the information I needed.
all.zh: 我安排了和三個部門的會議，蒐集所需的資訊。

all.en: My mentor reviewed my draft plan and praised my careful attention to details.
all.zh: 我的導師審閱了我的草案，稱讚我對細節的用心。

all.en: She suggested only two small changes and encouraged me to trust my instincts.
all.zh: 她只提出兩個小修改建議，並鼓勵我相信自己的直覺。

all.en: By Friday, I had presented the finished plan to my team leader successfully.
all.zh: 到星期五，我已順利向組長簡報完成的計畫。

all.en: He approved the proposal on the spot and assigned three colleagues to support me.
all.zh: 他當場批准了提案，並指派三位同事協助我。

all.en: That weekend, I treated myself to a long hike with my favorite playlist.
all.zh: 那個週末，我犒賞自己去走了一趟長途健行，配上最愛的歌單。

all.en: After one month, I no longer felt like a stranger in my own office.
all.zh: 一個月後，我在自己的辦公室裡再也不覺得像個陌生人。

all.en: I started giving advice to the newest hire, just as my mentor once did.
all.zh: 我開始給最新進員工建議，就像當初我的導師對我那樣。

all.en: By the third month, I was given my own small mentee fresh out of university.
all.zh: 到了第三個月，我有了自己第一位剛從大學畢業的學員。

all.en: She arrived on Monday with eyes wide open and a brand-new spiral notebook.
all.zh: 她星期一報到時眼睛睜得大大的，手裡拿著一本全新的活頁筆記本。

all.en: I remembered my own first day and made sure she had a warm welcome lunch.
all.zh: 我想起自己第一天的情景，特地安排了一頓溫暖的歡迎午餐。

all.en: I told her honestly that asking too many questions was always better than too few.
all.zh: 我老實告訴她，問太多問題永遠比問太少要好。

all.en: She wrote down the unspoken office rules just as I had once written them down.
all.zh: 她抄下那些不成文的辦公室規矩，就像當年的我那樣。

all.en: On her third afternoon, she shadowed me on a real call with a difficult client.
all.zh: 在她報到的第三天下午，她跟著我旁聽了一通棘手客戶的電話。

all.en: Afterward, she asked careful questions about tone, patience, and how to stay calm.
all.zh: 通話結束後，她仔細請教關於語氣、耐心，以及如何保持冷靜的細節。

all.en: By Friday, she could already name most teammates and find the printer without help.
all.zh: 到了星期五，她已能叫出大多數同事的名字，並且自己找到印表機。

all.en: Over the weekend, she sent me a quiet thank-you message with a small succulent emoji.
all.zh: 週末時，她傳了一則安靜的感謝訊息給我，附上一顆小小的多肉植物表情。

all.en: I realized mentoring others had taught me more than any onboarding course ever could.
all.zh: 我體會到，指導別人讓我學到的東西，遠超過任何新人訓練課程。

all.en: Six months later, my mentee was confidently leading her own small client project.
all.zh: 六個月後，我的學員已能自信地帶領自己負責的小型客戶專案。

all.en: She thanked me in front of the whole team during her project closing meeting.
all.zh: 她在專案結案會議上，當著全團隊的面向我致謝。

all.en: I felt the same warmth my mentor had once felt when I delivered my first solo plan.
all.zh: 我感受到當年我交出第一份獨立方案時，我的導師所感受到的同樣溫暖。

all.en: Our team grew steadily, and we welcomed three more fresh graduates the following summer.
all.zh: 我們團隊穩定成長，隔年夏天又迎來了三位剛畢業的新人。

all.en: I created a small handbook of unspoken office rules for every newcomer to read.
all.zh: 我為每位新人編寫了一本小手冊，列出辦公室裡的不成文規矩。

all.en: It included tips on the printer, the boss's coffee mug, and the best lunch spots.
all.zh: 內容涵蓋印表機、老闆的咖啡杯，以及最棒的午餐地點等小撇步。

all.en: My original mentor saw the handbook and laughed warmly at how far I had come.
all.zh: 我當年的導師看到這本手冊，溫暖地笑了，感嘆我已走了好長一段路。

all.en: She invited me to dinner and gave me a brand-new succulent for my growing collection.
all.zh: 她邀我吃晚餐，並送我一盆全新的多肉植物，加入我日漸增多的收藏。

all.en: That night, I placed it beside the original one I had received on my very first day.
all.zh: 那天晚上，我把它擺在報到第一天收到的那盆多肉植物旁邊。

all.en: I realized that good onboarding had quietly grown into a culture worth protecting forever.
all.zh: 我體會到，良好的新人訓練已悄悄成長為一份值得永遠守護的文化。

all.en: Three years later, I was promoted into the human resources team responsible for designing global onboarding programs.
all.zh: 三年後，我升任到人力資源團隊，負責設計全球的新人入職計畫。

all.en: I rewrote our handbook with fresh photos, simple steps, and a small welcome message in seven languages.
all.zh: 我重新編寫了手冊，加入新照片、簡單的步驟，並用七種語言寫了一段小小的歡迎訊息。

all.en: Every new hire now received a digital onboarding map alongside a printed quick start booklet.
all.zh: 現在每位新進員工都會收到一份數位入職地圖，搭配一本紙本的快速上手手冊。

all.en: Our overseas teams praised the new program as one of the warmest welcomes in our entire company history.
all.zh: 海外團隊稱這份新計畫為公司史上最溫暖的歡迎之一。

all.en: I started a small podcast where former new hires shared their first month stories with current new joiners.
all.zh: 我開了一個小播客，邀請從前的新人和現在的新進員工分享他們入職第一個月的故事。

all.en: My original mentor became one of my favorite guests, sharing her decade of warm mentorship memories.
all.zh: 我最初的那位導師成了我最喜歡的來賓之一，分享了她十年來溫馨的師徒回憶。

all.en: The succulents on my desk had quietly grown into a small green forest beside the company handbook shelf.
all.zh: 我桌上的多肉植物已經悄悄長成一片小綠林，靜靜地立在公司手冊書架旁邊。

all.en: On my next work anniversary, I gave a small succulent to every member of my growing team.
all.zh: 下一次工作週年紀念日，我送了每位團隊成員一盆小小的多肉植物。

all.en: Each plant carried a tiny tag with the words A small welcome that hopes to grow forever beside you.
all.zh: 每盆植物都掛著一張小吊牌，上面寫著「一份希望永遠陪你成長的小小歡迎」。

all.en: I realized that onboarding had truly become a culture passed gently from one heart to the next.
all.zh: 我體會到入職培訓真的已成為一種文化，從一顆心溫柔地傳遞到下一顆心。

all.en: The following spring, my company invited me to share our onboarding philosophy at an international HR summit.
all.zh: 隔年春天，公司邀請我在一場國際人資高峰會上分享我們的入職哲學。

all.en: I flew to Tokyo with a small green succulent carefully wrapped inside my carry-on travel bag.
all.zh: 我飛往東京，隨身行李裡小心翼翼地包著一盆小綠多肉植物。

all.en: At the summit, hundreds of human resources leaders listened as I described our seven-language welcome booklet.
all.zh: 在高峰會上，數百位人資領導者聆聽我介紹我們的七語歡迎手冊。

all.en: Many attendees came forward afterward, asking for permission to adapt our small handbook for their own offices.
all.zh: 會後許多與會者上前詢問，希望能在他們的辦公室調整使用我們的小手冊。

all.en: I gave permission gladly, asking only that each company keep one small succulent on every new hire's desk.
all.zh: 我欣然同意，只請求每家公司在每位新人桌上放一盆小小的多肉植物。

all.en: By the end of the summit, I had received emails from companies in twelve different countries and regions.
all.zh: 高峰會結束時，我已收到來自十二個不同國家和地區公司的電子郵件。

all.en: A small German firm sent photographs of their first welcome day, complete with tiny succulents lined neatly on every desk.
all.zh: 一家小型德國公司寄來他們首場迎新日的照片，每張桌上整齊地擺著小小的多肉植物。

all.en: I forwarded the photos to my original mentor, who replied simply with three small green plant emojis.
all.zh: 我把照片轉寄給我最初的導師，她只回了三個小小的綠色植物表情。

all.en: On the long flight home, I opened my notebook and started drafting a global onboarding curriculum together.
all.zh: 在漫長的歸途班機上，我打開筆記本，開始草擬一份全球入職共通課程。

all.en: I realized that one small succulent and one warm welcome could quietly travel halfway around the entire world.
all.zh: 我體會到，一盆小小的多肉植物和一份溫暖的歡迎，能悄悄地走遍半個地球。

all.en: The following winter, I began drafting our first ever global onboarding training certification for line managers.
all.zh: 隔年冬天，我開始草擬公司有史以來第一份提供給直屬主管的全球入職訓練認證。

all.en: Every manager would learn how to greet new hires warmly, listen carefully, and offer gentle weekly check-ins.
all.zh: 每位主管都會學習如何溫暖地迎接新人、細心傾聽，以及進行溫柔的每週關懷。

all.en: We piloted the certification in three regional offices, and feedback from new joiners was overwhelmingly positive.
all.zh: 我們在三個區域辦公室試行這項認證，新進員工的回饋極為正面。

all.en: Within one year, more than four hundred managers worldwide had earned the warm welcome certification badge.
all.zh: 一年之內，全球已有超過四百位主管獲得「溫暖歡迎」認證徽章。

all.en: Some managers even started small succulent giveaways inside their own meeting rooms during weekly team rituals.
all.zh: 有些主管甚至在每週團隊儀式中，在自家的會議室裡分送小小的多肉植物。

all.en: My original mentor flew across the country to attend our first global certification graduation ceremony in person.
all.zh: 我最初的那位導師飛越國境，親自出席我們首屆全球認證的結業典禮。

all.en: She gave a short speech about how culture grew quietly through every small plant placed on every new desk.
all.zh: 她發表了一段簡短演講，談論文化如何透過每張新桌上的一盆小植物悄悄成長。

all.en: Several managers wept gently as she described her decade of mentoring countless nervous first-day employees.
all.zh: 當她描述自己十年來指導無數第一天上班緊張新人的故事時，幾位主管輕輕落淚。

all.en: I closed the ceremony by placing a tiny succulent on every certified manager's chair before they rose to leave.
all.zh: 我以一個小儀式結束典禮：在每位獲認證主管的椅子上放上一盆小多肉，才讓他們起身離去。

all.en: Walking out together, we agreed that warm onboarding had quietly become our most powerful global team language.
all.zh: 我們一起走出會場時都同意，溫暖的新人入職已悄悄成為我們最強大的全球團隊共通語言。

all.en: The morning after the graduation ceremony, I came in to find that someone had quietly watered every succulent on my desk over the weekend.
all.zh: 結業典禮的隔天早上，我發現週末有人悄悄把我桌上每一盆多肉植物都澆了水。

all.en: A small thank-you note from a junior team member admitted she had been worried I would forget them during my busy travel weeks.
all.zh: 一位資淺同事留下的小謝卡上承認，她擔心我在到處出差的忙碌幾週裡會忘了它們。

all.en: I laughed and gave her a small succulent of her own, knowing she had quietly become the next quiet keeper of our culture.
all.zh: 我笑了笑，送了她一盆專屬的小多肉，明白她已悄悄成為我們文化下一位安靜的守護者。

all.en: Our German partner office mailed me a handwritten postcard with a photo of their welcome corner, now overflowing with tiny green plants.
all.zh: 我們在德國的合作辦公室寄來一張手寫明信片，附上他們歡迎角落的照片，現在已被小小的綠色植物填滿。

all.en: On the back, they had written a single sentence in shaky English: your succulent told us we were finally home.
all.zh: 明信片背面用顫顫巍巍的英文寫著一句話：「你的多肉告訴我們，我們終於到家了。」

all.en: I framed that postcard and hung it directly above my desk, replacing an older certificate I had once been so proud to display.
all.zh: 我把那張明信片裱框，掛在我桌子正上方，取代了一張我曾經很自豪展示的舊證書。

all.en: My original mentor sent me a video of her own retirement garden, which now featured a whole shelf of succulents grown from my early gifts.
all.zh: 我最初的導師寄來她退休花園的影片，現在有一整層架的多肉植物，全是從我早期送的禮物繁衍而來。

all.en: I started a small lunchtime tradition where new joiners told one story about their childhood plants while eating with our team.
all.zh: 我開始一個小小的午餐傳統，請新進員工在與團隊用餐時分享一個關於童年植物的小故事。

all.en: One newcomer shared that her late grandfather had once grown bonsai trees on a tiny rooftop in a faraway coastal town.
all.zh: 一位新人分享她已故的祖父曾在遙遠沿海小鎮一個小小的屋頂上種盆栽。

all.en: I quietly added a tiny bonsai tree to her desk the very next morning, with no note and no explanation, just a quiet welcome.
all.zh: 隔天早上我悄悄在她的桌上放了一棵小盆栽，沒有留言也沒有解釋，只是一份安靜的歡迎。
